key_departures.impact: Starting left guard signed a 4-year, $61M deal ($15M+/yr) with the Saints. Buffalo could not compete financially given cap constraints. Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M) is the likely replacement — a significant downgrade from a starter who helped anchor the NFL's top run-blocking unit.

Traded to Raiders for a 6th-round pick (plus Bills sent a 7th). Johnson was the starting nickel corner and a key piece of the Bills' secondary. Departure was deliberate (scheme change under Leonhard) but creates a hole at nickel that needed to be filled.
Two-year deal with Titans. Departing backup QB behind Josh Allen. Low-impact loss — Josh Allen has not missed a start since his rookie season. Bills quickly replaced with Kyle Allen.
Starting fullback signed 2-year deal (max $12M, $6M gtd) with Patriots. Loss of a key run-game piece and special teams contributor, but fullback is a niche role and limited draft/FA market impact.

Cap casualty cut to clear space. Was the Week 1 starter at safety in 2025 despite being a mid-tier player. Departure — combined with losses of Jordan Poyer, Damar Hamlin, and Darnell Savage (all UFA departures) — left the entire safety room needing to be rebuilt.

One-year prove-it deal (1yr/$12.6M) in 2025 produced only 5 sacks. Not re-signed. Bills ranked 27th in pass rush win rate in 2025. Departure leaves a significant EDGE hole that was the team's stated top defensive need entering free agency.
Depth/rotational DB signed 2-year deal with Bears. Minor secondary depth loss.
Signed 1-year deal with Steelers. Part of the full safety room overhaul — had a spot-start role in 2025 but not a core piece.
Rotational EDGE rusher who did not return. Adds to the thin edge depth behind Gregory Rousseau.

contract.notes: Acquired via trade. Bills sent 2026 2nd-round pick (#60 overall) to Chicago; received Moore + 2026 5th-round pick (#165). Bills also guaranteed $15.5M of Moore's 2028 salary as part of trade. Contract runs through 2029 at $24.5M/yr. Bills converted salary to lower 2026 cap hit. Guarantees estimated from Spotrac/reported figures.

signings.analysis: The Bills' most impactful acquisition of the offseason. Moore reunites with HC Joe Brady, under whom he had back-to-back 1,100-yard seasons in Carolina. At $24.5M AAV he's at the upper starter tier for WR. The 4-year remaining deal with significant guarantees signals a long-term commitment — WR1 need is fully addressed. Bills cost: their 2026 2nd-round pick (#60), which meaningfully reduces draft capital. Moore had a down 2025 (50 catches, 682 yards) but context matters — poor usage in Chicago.

contract.notes: Max value $52.5M with incentives. Per NFL Network Insiders Rapoport/Pelissero/Garafolo. Confirmed by Spotrac and agent.

signings.analysis: Three-year deal with $29M guaranteed is a real starter investment — this is not a bridge move. Chubb had 8.5 sacks in 2025 for Miami, two Pro Bowl selections career, and fills the void left by Bosa's departure. At $14.5M AAV he is below the market for elite EDGE (sub-starter tier pricing for a player with starter upside). The discount stems from durability concerns (torn ACL in both knees). He joins Gregory Rousseau (already under contract) to form the starting edge tandem. 'Partially addressed' rather than 'fully' due to health history and the absence of a proven second pass rusher behind him — draft still warranted at EDGE for depth/developmental player.

contract.notes: Per ESPN's Adam Schefter, confirmed by team. Spotrac lists $31.44M guaranteed; Buffalo Rumblings detail $26.2M gtd at signing + $5.24M vesting. Re-signed before free agency opened.

signings.analysis: McGovern is the starting center and one of the best at his position — PFF's 9th-best center in 2025. Four-year deal with $32M guaranteed is a strong long-term commitment at C. This resolves the center need completely. However, David Edwards (LG) simultaneously departed to the Saints on a 4yr/$61M deal, leaving the left guard spot vacant. IOL remains a need specifically at LG — Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M) is a below-starter stopgap. Center: fully addressed. Guard: unaddressed. Net: partial.

contract.notes: Base deal $15.75M per Spotrac; ESPN's Jeremy Fowler reported up to $21M with incentives. $10.12M guaranteed ($7.875M fully guaranteed at signing). Confirmed by Spotrac and OTC.

signings.analysis: Alford is the direct Taron Johnson replacement as the Bills' nickel cornerback under new DC Jim Leonhard. Three-year deal shows commitment, and the $10M guarantee is real money. However, at $5.25M AAV this is well below the CB starter market — essentially a low-starter/high-depth price. He's a scheme fit (vast majority of snaps at nickel with Atlanta) but has question marks (ranked 94th among CBs by PFF in 2025). The Bills still have outside CB needs with the losses of Johnson, Lewis, and other secondary pieces. CB need is partially addressed at nickel, but the outside CB room (Kaiir Elam, Taron Johnson gone) remains a legitimate draft target.

contract.notes: One-year deal worth up to $6M per agent and ESPN's Adam Schefter. Guarantees estimated.

signings.analysis: One-year deal is a bridge/prove-it structure — this is not a long-term answer at safety. Gardner-Johnson is a veteran who had one of his best seasons in 2024 and will compete to start opposite Cole Bishop. With the entire safety room (Poyer, Hamlin, Savage, Rapp) having departed, this is necessary but not sufficient. The 1-year structure means S remains a real need for the draft and/or later FA additions. Partially addressed.

contract.notes: One-year deal. Initial reports cited $7M/yr (Stone initially signed two-year deal worth $7M annually then reworked to reduce salary by $1.5M per ESPN). Final structure approximately 1yr/$5.5M based on renegotiation reported by ESPN's Alaina Getzenberg.

signings.analysis: One-year depth add to a completely overhauled safety room. Stone had a career-high 104 tackles in 2025 but also led the NFL in missed tackles (26). His role — depth/competition with Gardner-Johnson and Cole Bishop — makes this a minimal move on the need needle. Safety is still a draft priority.

contract.notes: Three-year deal per ESPN's Jeremy Fowler. Buffalo Rumblings reports $6.49M total, $2.53M gtd at signing. Max value $7.5M.

signings.analysis: Core special teams ace retained at near-minimum money. Franklin logged only 6 defensive snaps in 2025 — his value is entirely in third-phase football. This does not address the starting safety need. No impact on needs picture.

contract.notes: Reworked deal from bloated $17M cap hit. Spotrac: 3yr/$20M, $13.46M gtd. Was restructured to lower 2026 cap hit by ~$8.5M vs. old deal. Contract through 2028.

signings.analysis: Knox was already on the roster — this restructure keeps him at a dramatically reduced cap number. TE was not a need entering FA; Knox + Dalton Kincaid + Jackson Hawes give Buffalo arguably one of the NFL's best TE rooms. This move preserves a strength rather than addressing a weakness. No needs impact.

contract.notes: Per ESPN's Adam Schefter, confirmed by team. Max value $6.1M. $1.8M guaranteed per NFLTradeRumors.

signings.analysis: Veteran backup re-signing directly replaces departed Trubisky. Josh Allen hasn't missed a start since his rookie year (122-game streak). Kyle Allen is a familiarity/comfort pick for both Josh Allen and HC Joe Brady. No change to the QB need picture.

contract.notes: One-year, $2.5M deal per CBS Sports FA tracker.

signings.analysis: Depth IDL signing at minimum-adjacent money. Collier is a former first-round pick (2019, Seahawks) with modest production. One-year deal signals a rotational/camp competition role. Does not address the IDL starter need but adds roster depth behind Ed Oliver under the new Leonhard 3-4 scheme.

contract.notes: One-year, $2.5M per Buffalo Rumblings. Was a restricted free agent.

signings.analysis: Anderson is the frontrunner to replace David Edwards at left guard per beat reporters, but at $2.5M on a one-year deal this is a clear stopgap. He has experience as an extra lineman/fill-in but has not been tested as a full-time starter. Minimally addresses the LG hole left by Edwards' departure.

contract.notes: One-year, $1.195M per D&C tracker. Near veteran minimum.

signings.analysis: Former 2nd-round pick retained at near-minimum. Played 6 games with the Bills in 2025. Developmental depth piece under Leonhard's new 3-4 front. Does not change IDL need picture.

contract.notes: One-year deal per team announcement. Terms estimated at ST-level value.

signings.analysis: Re-signed punter who had a solid 2025 season (47.4 net yards, 18 inside-the-20). Special teams continuity signing. No draft/FA need implications.

contract.notes: RFA tender at $3.52M per Buffalo Rumblings.

signings.analysis: UDFA-origin swing tackle retained via RFA tender. Developmental OL depth. No needs impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: DJ Moore trade (4yr/$98M remaining, $24.5M AAV) adds a proven WR1 who reunites with HC Joe Brady — under whom Moore had two 1,100+ yard seasons. Khalil Shakir, Keon Coleman, and Joshua Palmer provide additional depth. WR is no longer a premium draft need. At most a day-3 add for developmental depth.

Bradley Chubb signing (3yr/$43.5M, $29M gtd) is a real starter investment paired with Gregory Rousseau already under contract. EDGE goes from critical hole to addressed-but-thin. Chubb's ACL history and age (turns 30 in June) plus the lack of proven depth behind Rousseau/Chubb means a developmental or mid-round EDGE in the draft remains worthwhile. Drops from tier 1 critical to tier 2.

McGovern re-sign (4yr/$52M) fully resolves the center. But David Edwards' departure to the Saints (4yr/$61M) creates a new left guard hole. Alec Anderson (1yr/$2.5M) is a below-starter stopgap. Net result: IOL need stays at tier 2, shifted entirely to LG. Should be addressed in rounds 2-4 of the draft.

Dee Alford (3yr/$15.75M base) adequately addresses nickel CB — the specific need created by trading Taron Johnson. However, outside CB depth remains thin following the departures of Taron Johnson and Cam Lewis. Kaiir Elam is the primary outside CB. CB at outside/boundary remains a tier 2 need for the draft.

The entire safety room was overhauled: Poyer, Hamlin, Savage all departed UFA; Rapp was cut. CJ Gardner-Johnson (1yr/$6M) and Geno Stone (1yr/$5.5M) are both on one-year bridge deals. Cole Bishop is the only long-term piece at the position. With two one-year contracts and zero multi-year commitments at safety beyond Bishop, S remains a tier 2 draft priority — ideally rounds 2-3.

Ed Oliver (restructured, under contract) remains. LJ Collier (1yr/$2.5M) and Phidarian Mathis (1yr/$1.2M) add depth but neither is a starter-quality piece in Leonhard's new 3-4. IDL depth remains a day-2/3 draft target. Need unchanged at tier 3.

No LB additions in free agency. Matt Milano's void year created $11M in dead cap but he is no longer under contract. The new Leonhard 3-4 scheme specifically requires off-ball LBs. This remains an unaddressed need and a legitimate day-2 draft target.

Dion Dawkins (LT) and Spencer Brown (RT, restructured) remain the starting tackles. Van Demark is the only backup tackle under contract. OT depth is thin but not a starter need. Day-3 developmental tackle would be prudent.

key_additions.headline: Reunites with HC Joe Brady; two-time 1,100-yard receiver immediately becomes WR1 and fully resolves Bills' biggest offensive need
Two-time Pro Bowl pass rusher (8.5 sacks in 2025) fills the Joey Bosa void; pairs with Rousseau to give Buffalo a legitimate starting EDGE tandem
Pro Bowl center locked up long-term before free agency; anchor of the Bills' run-blocking unit stays put for 4 more years
Nickel cornerback slides into Taron Johnson's slot role; three-year deal signals real investment in Leonhard's new scheme fit
Veteran safety brought in to compete for starting role opposite Cole Bishop amid complete overhaul of the Bills' safety room
Career-high 104 tackles in 2025; second veteran safety added to rebuilt secondary as depth/competition alongside Gardner-Johnson and Bishop
Knox's $17M cap hit restructured to ~$8.5M in 2026; tight end tandem with Kincaid and Hawes fully intact through 2028

key_losses.headline: Starting left guard cashes in on the open market; Bills couldn't compete at $15M+/yr, leaving the most pressing remaining hole on the roster at LG

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Las Vegas Raiders (Bills received 6th-round pick)
key_losses.headline: Starting nickel corner traded away as part of Leonhard's defensive overhaul; Dee Alford signed as the direct replacement
One-year experiment at EDGE produced only 5 sacks; not re-signed after Bills moved on to Bradley Chubb on a 3-year deal
Cap casualty cut triggered a complete safety room overhaul; Bills also lost Poyer, Hamlin, and Savage to UFA, forcing a full secondary rebuild
Backup QB departs for Tennessee; Kyle Allen signed on a 2-year deal as the familiar replacement behind Josh Allen
